---
title: "Edward Hoffmann"
pagetitle: "Edward Hoffmann"
toc: false
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
---

::: {.hero .hero-with-photo}

::: {.hero-photo}

![](./images/profile.png){alt="Edward Hoffmann"}

:::

::: {.hero-copy}

Currently working as a research and teaching assistant at UC Berkeley and as a quantitative researcher for Helix. 

I'm passionate about the theoretical foundations of decision making and learning. 

My academic background includes graduate coursework in optimization, game theory, and classical ML. Economics and statistics (UC Davis, '25). 

:::

:::

## Interests {#interests}


- Decision making under uncertainty: algorithmic game theory and reinforcement learning.
- Deep learning dynamics: structural and geometric properties of learning in neural networks.
- System dynamics & control: ergodicity, optimal control, and resource allocation.

## Projects {#projects}

#### [A Bird’s-Eye View: Hawkes Processes in Texting Data](posts/hawkes-text-messages.qmd)
Inspired by Hawkes processes in statistical mechanics, wanted to apply it to a different self-exciting process. Modeled the arrival of text messages as a Hawkes process. Tried various kernels and various variable background rate methods to try and find the best fit.

## Upcoming {#upcoming}
#### Functional Dimension as a Motivation for MUON
Explore possible connections between [functional dimension](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2209.04036) and [MUON](https://kellerjordan.github.io/posts/muon/) for ReLU neural networks. Will be exploring this with toy models similar to the work by Dr. Grigsby.

#### Learned Geometry of "Thinking" in Transformers

Explore how depth and residual structure shape internal representations during training—connecting ideas from [Florentin Guth](https://florentinguth.github.io/publication/rainbow/) on layer-wise distribution learning with [David Noel Ng](https://dnhkng.github.io/posts/rys-ii/) on layer-wise "thinking" in deep networks. Hoping I can investigate this with local compute.
